title: alchemilla gracilis var. palustris
intro: Alchemilla gracilis var. palustris, also known as marsh lady's mantle, is a low-growing, perennial herb belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae) and native to wetlands in Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated skin, wounds, and digestive issues. The health benefits of marsh lady's mantle include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and alleviating symptoms of diarrhea, digestive problems, and urinary tract issues. Marsh lady's mantle can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in salves and creams to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
